16.reason: [If p->left is NULL, calling f(p->left, h+1) will dereference a null pointer, causing undefined behavior or crash.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [If p->left is NULL, calling f(p->left, h+1) will dereference a null pointer, causing undefined behavior or crash.]

17.reason: [If p->right is NULL, calling f(p->right, h+1) will dereference a null pointer, causing undefined behavior or crash.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [If p->right is NULL, calling f(p->right, h+1) will dereference a null pointer, causing undefined behavior or crash.]

77.reason: [If the input n is 0, root will be NULL, and passing NULL to function f will cause a null pointer dereference. Also, even for n>=1, recursive calls in f may pass NULL pointers without checking.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [If the input n is 0, root will be NULL, and passing NULL to function f will cause a null pointer dereference. Also, even for n>=1, recursive calls in f may pass NULL pointers without checking.]
